question: Will the price of Solana be above $80 on April 19?

fragility_points: A sharp downside move before Apr 19 could quickly erase the modest cushion over $80.
ET-to-UTC settlement-minute mapping needs care when the market actually resolves.
This run used daily Binance data for contextual verification rather than one-minute historical settlement-window data.
key_assumptions: Recent Binance SOL/USDT trading in the low-to-mid 80s broadly persists into Apr 19.
There is no major crypto-wide downside shock before the settlement minute.
Binance market functioning and candle mapping remain normal at settlement.
main_logical_chain: Confirm the contract resolves using the Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle close on Apr 19.
Check recent Binance venue-specific SOL/USDT price distribution around the $80 threshold.
Use that outside-view prior to conclude Yes is favored, but haircut heavily for single-minute settlement risk.
Compare the resulting estimate to the 91.5% market price and conclude the market is too confident.
payload.main_thesis: Yes is more likely than No because recent Binance SOL/USDT trading has mostly been above $80, but the market's 91.5% price overstates confidence for a single-minute noon ET threshold contract.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
My base-rate view is that Yes is more likely than No, but not nearly as likely as the market implies.
payload.source_quality_view: Good for a medium-difficulty run: direct market rules plus direct Binance venue data, but the contextual Binance check used daily rather than exact one-minute history.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact 12:00 ET one-minute close, so timing noise matters more than daily-level intuition.
Recent Binance intraday lows sometimes fell below $80 even when daily closes finished above it.
strongest_supports: Binance daily closes were above $80 on 13 of 15 completed days in the Apr 1-15 sample.
Retrieved spot context remained in the mid-80s, leaving cushion over the threshold.
The resolution venue and contextual price source are aligned on Binance SOL/USDT.
payload.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves on a single one-minute close at 12:00 ET on Apr 19, 2026, so ordinary intraday volatility can dominate the outcome.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact Binance UI candle labeling at the ET noon mapping was not independently verified beyond the contract rules.
Intraday distribution near noon ET on comparable nearby days was not directly sampled.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move up if SOL carries a larger cushion above $80 near settlement or if direct intraday noon-window evidence shows limited downside risk; I would move down if SOL loses the low-80s zone or broader crypto turns risk-off.

fragility_points: High-beta crypto can move more than 6% in a short window.
A Solana outage, exploit, or exchange/liquidity issue would matter disproportionately near settlement.
key_assumptions: No sharp negative crypto or Solana-specific catalyst hits before the April 19 noon ET print.
Binance remains a stable usable resolution surface and the EDT-to-16:00-UTC mapping is correct.
main_logical_chain: The contract settles on the Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on April 19.
Current and recent direct Binance prices are above 80, so Yes is favored absent a fresh downside catalyst.
Because the market is pricing about 91.5% and the outcome depends on one exact minute, residual timing risk keeps my estimate lower at 84%.
payload.main_thesis: SOL is likely to finish above $80 on the relevant Binance noon-ET minute candle, but the market overstates certainty because one-minute timing risk still matters.
quote_anchors: Yes requires the final Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET 1m close to be strictly greater than 80
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality direct resolution evidence from Binance, with limited but appropriate independence because Binance is the governing settlement source; source-of-truth ambiguity is low after timezone verification.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact minute candle, so a weekend selloff or Solana-specific shock could still push the close below 80.
The current cushion above 80 is meaningful but not so large that downside volatility can be ignored.
strongest_supports: Binance spot check at assignment time showed SOLUSDT at 85.38, already above the threshold.
Recent noon-ET-equivalent Binance 1m candles on Apr 13-15 all closed above 80.
payload.timing_relevance: This is mainly a timing-risk case: the key repricing catalyst is any downside shock before the exact noon ET print, not a bullish information release.
unresolved_ambiguities: No single identified scheduled catalyst dominates; the broader crypto tape remains the main near-term driver.
Exact weekend risk sentiment before settlement is inherently uncertain.
payload.what_would_change_view: A drop back toward or below 80, a broad crypto risk-off move, or credible Solana/Binance operational issues before settlement would push the estimate lower.

fragility_points: Short-horizon crypto volatility can erase the cushion quickly.
A late selloff or wick exactly into the noon ET minute could flip the outcome.
key_assumptions: SOL only needs to hold above an already-cleared threshold rather than rally much further.
No major crypto-wide or Solana-specific negative catalyst hits before the settlement minute.
Binance noon-ET 1-minute candle mapping is straightforward and remains the effective source of truth.
main_logical_chain: Start from the 91.5% market-implied prior.
Check the governing venue and contract mechanics directly on Binance / Polymarket rules.
Observe that SOL is already comfortably above 80, so persistence is the main requirement.
Haircut the market slightly because exact-minute settlement and crypto volatility leave a nontrivial path to No.
Land at 86%, roughly agreeing with the market on direction but not degree.
payload.main_thesis: The market is directionally right because Binance already has SOL materially above 80, but exact-minute settlement and normal crypto volatility justify a modest haircut versus the 91.5% market price.
quote_anchors: Polymarket rule: Yes if Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 ET closes higher than 80
payload.source_quality_view: High-confidence primary source-of-truth on rules and venue, with one useful secondary cross-check; independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low.
strongest_disconfirmers: A ~6% drawdown over several days is plausible in crypto.
The contract settles on one exact 1-minute Binance close, not a daily average.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance SOL/USDT read was about 85.27, already roughly $5.27 above strike.
Recent Binance 1-minute closes were clustered in the mid-85s rather than barely above 80.
CoinGecko cross-check also showed SOL around 85.2, supporting the direct read.
payload.timing_relevance: The contract resolves at the Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close on April 19, 2026, so exact-minute timing matters materially.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much realized volatility SOL will see between now and April 19.
Whether any weekend-style or event-driven move hits the exact settlement minute.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move lower if SOL loses the 82-83 area or if a fresh Binance check near expiry shows the cushion over 80 has mostly vanished; I would move toward the market if SOL remains comfortably above 83-84 into April 18-19.

fragility_points: A 6%-7% drawdown before resolution is plausible in crypto.
Minor UI/API settlement-surface ambiguity if Binance display and API ever diverge.
key_assumptions: SOL keeps enough cushion above 80 through Apr. 19 noon ET.
Binance SOL/USDT remains operationally normal and aligned with broader spot markets.
No crypto-wide drawdown pushes SOL below the strike at the settlement minute.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket rules make Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close the governing outcome test.
Current Binance price and recent Binance candles put SOL above 80 with modest cushion.
Because only one exact minute matters, ordinary crypto volatility justifies discounting the market's extreme confidence.
That leaves Yes as likely, but below the market-implied probability.
payload.main_thesis: SOL above 80 on Apr. 19 noon ET is likely, but the market overstates confidence because exact-minute Binance settlement creates meaningful path risk.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
My estimate is 81% Yes versus a market-implied probability of 91.5%.
payload.source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high because contract rules and Binance-native price data were checked directly; independence is moderate because Coinbase was only a contextual cross-check.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact minute, so a brief dip at noon ET is enough to lose.
The cushion over 80 is real but not huge for a volatile altcoin over several days.
Extreme market pricing may underweight narrow settlement and path risk.
strongest_supports: Binance spot was about 85.3 at review time, already above the strike.
Last 10 Binance daily closes checked were all above 80.
Recent hourly Binance closes stayed roughly in an 81.7-87.3 band.
payload.timing_relevance: High: this market resolves on the Apr. 19, 2026 12:00 ET Binance one-minute close, which maps to 16:00 UTC during daylight saving time.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether the market should be priced closer to high-70s/low-80s or upper-80s given current cushion.
How much practical settlement ambiguity exists between Binance page UI and public API values.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move toward the market if SOL holds comfortably above 82-83 for another 1-2 days; I would move down if SOL slides back toward 80-82, broader crypto weakens, or Binance-specific anomalies appear.

fragility_points: If SOL rallies further into the high-80s, the below-market variant weakens quickly.
This view relies on ordinary downside path risk persisting into settlement.
key_assumptions: The current ~$5 cushion above 80 is helpful but not secure enough for >90% confidence over ~3.5 days.
The exact noon ET 1-minute close rule adds path risk beyond a simple end-of-day threshold framing.
main_logical_chain: The contract settles to Binance SOL/USDT 1-minute close at 12:00 ET on April 19, not to any broader daily average or cross-exchange price.
Current Binance spot near 85.3 makes Yes the directional favorite.
But a ~6% cushion over several days in a volatile crypto asset is not large enough to justify the market's ~91.5% confidence.
Therefore Yes is still more likely than No, but below market confidence at about 84%.
payload.main_thesis: Yes remains the base case because SOL is already above 80 on Binance, but the market likely overstates confidence because a ~6% cushion over several days is not enough to make an exact noon ET 1-minute close near-certain.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than ... 80.
payload.source_quality_view: Strong primary source quality because Binance is settlement-aligned, with medium evidence independence after adding CoinGecko as a contextual cross-check and low source-of-truth ambiguity.
strongest_disconfirmers: SOL is already more than $5 above 80, so a modestly bullish continuation would make Yes comfortable.
Low contract ambiguity means the main thesis depends mostly on volatility calibration rather than hidden rules.
strongest_supports: Binance SOLUSDT was around 85.28-85.30 at verification time, already above the threshold.
Recent Binance 1-minute closes and 24h change were favorable rather than deteriorating.
CoinGecko broadly confirmed the mid-85s price context.
payload.timing_relevance: Timing is central because the market resolves on one exact Binance 1-minute close at noon ET on April 19, so transient weakness at that minute is enough for No.
unresolved_ambiguities: Near-term realized volatility through April 19 was not deeply modeled in this run.
Intraday noon ET behavior closer to settlement could still matter.
payload.what_would_change_view: A larger price cushion, continued broad crypto strength, or evidence of lower-than-assumed volatility would push the estimate closer to the market; renewed weakness toward low-80s would push it lower.
